target.label: Shared Filtering for Skill and Subagent Tool Sets

rationale: P0 coverage is 100% (required: 100%), P1 coverage is 100% (target: 90%), overall coverage is 100% (minimum: 80%). All 8 acceptance criteria (AC1-AC8) are fully covered by 31 new passing unit tests (26 ATDD red-phase tests + 5 code-review regression fixes) plus extensive pre-existing regression protection (ToolRestrictionStackTests 28, ToolRegistryTests.filterTools_*, SkillLoaderTests.parseToolDeclarations_* 10 from 29.4, DefaultSubAgentSpawnerTests 29.2 filterTests 5). The filterToolsByDeclarations helper, ToolFilterDiagnostics/ToolFilterOptions carriers, DefaultSubAgentSpawner rewiring, executeSkill/executeSkillStream migration, assembleFullToolPool integration, SkillTool metadata enrichment, and ToolDeclaration.parse/fromToolNames tokenizer hoist all passed 3-layer adversarial code review with 3 in-scope correctness fixes applied (CRITICAL mixed-case MCP normalizedName casing asymmetry that broke AC3, HIGH declaration path not clearing legacy allowedTools causing double-filter regression, HIGH fromToolNames not filtering empty/whitespace tokens), each with a dedicated regression test. All 5 Epic 29 red lines verified honored: (1) ToolRegistry.filterTools signature unchanged, (2) no .task case added to ToolRestriction enum, (3) launcher stripping stays in DefaultSubAgentSpawner not in helper, (4) empty pool + diagnostics when all allowed unmatched (never unrestricted fallback), (5) sibling code paths consistent (executeSkill, executeSkillStream, assembleFullToolPool all route through applyAllowedDeclarations). Full suite at 5769/5769 passing (baseline 5738 -> +26 ATDD +5 review regression = +31). Story status done. No critical, high, medium, or low coverage gaps identified.

AC1.description: Helper function filterToolsByDeclarations exists in correct module (Types/ToolDeclaration.swift) with ToolFilterDiagnostics/ToolFilterOptions carriers, pure function with no cross-layer imports

AC2.description: Subagent tool pool filtered by declarations - allowed preserves only matching tools, disallowed overrides allowed, case-insensitive, nil/empty allowed returns all

AC3.description: MCP tool declaration matches without enum case (status .recognizedMCP, toolRestriction nil)

AC4.description: Declared-but-no-available-tool produces diagnostics.unmatchedDeclarations, NEVER falls back to unrestricted (single unmatched + all unmatched both yield empty pool)

AC5.description: Skill execution path (executeSkill/executeSkillStream) consumes skill.toolDeclarations, preserves MCP tools that legacy toolRestrictions path drops, falls back to toolRestrictions when declarations nil

AC6.description: SkillTool metadata exposes richer toolDeclarations (rawName/normalizedName/status/pattern/hasToolRestriction per declaration), backward compatible when nil

AC7.description: Backward compatibility - no regression on ToolRestrictionStack (28 tests), ExecuteSkill/ExecuteSkillStream, SkillTool, SkillLoader, DefaultSubAgentSpawner (29.2 filterTools), ToolRegistry.filterTools signature unchanged, 6 BuiltInSkills toolRestrictions inits unchanged
tests: Pre-existing regression suites (verified green): ToolRestrictionStackTests (28), ToolRegistryTests.filterTools_*, SkillLoaderTests.parseToolDeclarations_* (10), DefaultSubAgentSpawnerTests.filterTools_* 29.2 (5), ExecuteSkillTests/ExecuteSkillStreamTests existing

AC8.description: Build clean with zero new warnings and full test suite passes (baseline 5738 + 31 new = 5769)

critical.finding: MCP normalizedName casing asymmetry broke AC3 for mixed-case MCP names (mcp__GitHub__ListPRs never matched the lowercased available set)

high.finding: Declaration path did not clear options.allowedTools causing double-filter regression that dropped MCP tools when host had pre-set allowlist
fromToolNames did not filter empty/whitespace tokens producing phantom .unknown declarations

red_lines_verified_honored: ToolRegistry.filterTools (Tools/ layer) signature unchanged, not deleted
No .task case added to ToolRestriction enum - Task matched via knownClaudeCodeOnly string set
Launcher stripping stays in DefaultSubAgentSpawner, NOT in the helper (filterToolsByDeclarations contains zero launcher logic)
When allowed declarations all unmatched, helper returns EMPTY pool, never unrestricted fallback

Sibling code paths consistent - executeSkill, executeSkillStream, assembleFullToolPool all route through applyAllowedDeclarations -> filterToolsByDeclarations; both skill paths save/restore savedAllowedDeclarations
deferred_to_later_stories: Fine-grained Bash permission pattern enforcement (epic deferred item 5) - Story 29.7+
Restore race on options mutations during concurrent executeSkillStream (pre-existing for allowedTools, cloned for allowedToolDeclarations, MEDIUM deferred)
Stray ) and __ inside MCP tool names edge cases (MEDIUM deferred, requires parser semantics decisions)
patternDeclarations dedup by rawName and unmatchedDeclarations dedup (LOW cosmetic, deferred)
